metadata.name: DORA ICT Third-Party Provider Assessment
metadata.description: Comprehensive assessment questionnaire for ICT third-party service providers under DORA (Digital Operational Resilience Act) - Articles 28-30
metadata.scope: Financial entities and ICT third-party service providers

questions.question_text: Has your organization established and implemented comprehensive ICT third-party risk management processes in accordance with DORA Article 28(1)?
questions.description: DORA requires financial entities to manage ICT third-party risk as an integral part of their ICT risk management framework. Verify documented processes for identifying, assessing, and managing ICT third-party risks.
required_evidence: Risk register showing ICT third-party risks
Board approval of risk management framework

questions.question_text: Do you maintain a comprehensive process for identifying all ICT services provided to financial entities, including categorization by criticality?
questions.description: DORA Article 28(1) requires: Financial entities must identify and categorize all ICT third-party dependencies, distinguishing between critical and non-critical services.

questions.question_text: Do you conduct comprehensive risk assessments before entering into contractual arrangements for ICT services with financial entities?
questions.description: DORA Article 28(2) requires thorough risk assessment before engaging ICT third-party service providers, covering operational, security, and compliance risks.
required_evidence: Risk register showing ICT third-party risks
Board approval of risk management framework

questions.question_text: Have you established processes for ongoing monitoring and periodic reassessment of ICT third-party risks throughout the contractual relationship?
questions.description: Risk assessment is not a one-time activity. DORA requires continuous monitoring and periodic reassessment of third-party risks.
required_evidence: Risk register showing ICT third-party risks
Board approval of risk management framework

questions.question_text: Do you assess and report concentration risk arising from dependencies on single or multiple ICT third-party service providers?
questions.description: DORA Article 28(2) requires: Financial entities must identify and manage concentration risks, including dependencies on the same provider or interconnected providers.
required_evidence: Risk register showing ICT third-party risks
Board approval of risk management framework

questions.question_text: Are all ICT service arrangements with financial entities formalized through comprehensive written contractual agreements?
questions.description: DORA Article 28(3) mandates written contractual arrangements for all ICT services, clearly defining rights, obligations, and responsibilities.

questions.question_text: Do your contracts clearly define the roles, rights, and responsibilities of both parties, including data ownership and processing obligations?
questions.description: Contracts must explicitly define all parties' roles, responsibilities, data ownership, and processing obligations under GDPR and DORA.

questions.question_text: Have you established comprehensive exit strategies and transition plans for all ICT services provided to financial entities?
questions.description: DORA Article 28(4) requires detailed exit strategies to ensure business continuity and orderly transition in case of contract termination.

questions.question_text: Do your exit strategies include provisions for complete data extraction, transfer, and deletion in commonly used formats?
questions.description: DORA Article 28(4) requires: Exit plans must ensure data can be extracted and transferred in standard formats, with complete deletion after transition.

questions.question_text: Do your exit plans ensure continuity of service during transition periods without disruption to financial entity operations?
questions.description: DORA Article 28(4) requires: Exit strategies must minimize disruption and ensure operational continuity during provider transitions.

questions.question_text: Do your contracts include clear and complete descriptions of ICT services, including service levels, locations, and functions?
questions.description: DORA Article 29(1)(a) requires detailed service descriptions including locations, service levels, and specific functions performed.

questions.question_text: Do you disclose all locations where data is processed and stored, including subcontractor locations, in compliance with DORA requirements?
questions.description: DORA Article 29(1) requires: Contracts must specify all processing and storage locations, including those of subcontractors and cloud providers.

questions.question_text: Have you defined quantitative and qualitative service level targets with associated measurement mechanisms and reporting?
questions.description: DORA Article 29(1)(c) requires clear SLAs with measurable targets, monitoring mechanisms, and regular reporting.

questions.question_text: Do your SLAs specify minimum availability requirements, including RTO (Recovery Time Objective) and RPO (Recovery Point Objective)?
questions.description: DORA Article 29(1) requires: SLAs must define availability commitments and recovery objectives aligned with financial entity requirements.

questions.question_text: Do you grant financial entities and their competent authorities full access and audit rights to assess ICT risk management and service delivery?
questions.description: DORA Article 29(1)(d) mandates comprehensive audit rights for financial entities and their supervisory authorities.

questions.question_text: Do you provide evidence of independent third-party audits and certifications (e.g., SOC 2, ISO 27001) to demonstrate compliance with security standards?
questions.description: DORA Article 29(1) requires: Financial entities may require evidence of independent audits and certifications as part of their oversight.
required_evidence: Penetration test results from qualified assessors

questions.question_text: Do your contracts require prior written approval from financial entities before subcontracting critical ICT services?
questions.description: DORA Article 29(1)(e) requires financial entities to approve subcontracting arrangements for critical or important functions.

questions.question_text: Do you ensure that subcontractors are bound by the same contractual obligations and security requirements as apply to you?
questions.description: DORA Article 29(1) requires: All subcontracting arrangements must include flow-down clauses ensuring equivalent protection and obligations.

questions.question_text: Do your contracts include provisions for immediate notification of ICT-related incidents to financial entities?
questions.description: DORA Article 29(1)(f) requires prompt incident notification, including details on impact, root cause, and remediation.

questions.question_text: Have you established incident classification criteria aligned with DORA requirements to determine reporting thresholds?
questions.description: DORA Article 29(1) requires: Incidents must be classified by severity and impact to determine appropriate notification timelines.

questions.question_text: Do your contracts include provisions allowing financial entities to terminate for cause, including regulatory compliance failures?
questions.description: DORA Article 29(1)(g) requires termination clauses that protect financial entity interests, including regulatory non-compliance.
required_evidence: Business continuity plans for provider exit

questions.question_text: Do your contracts commit to providing comprehensive transition assistance during and after contract termination?
questions.description: DORA Article 29(1) requires: Exit clauses must include obligations to assist with orderly transition, knowledge transfer, and data migration.
required_evidence: Business continuity plans for provider exit

questions.question_text: Do your contracts specify comprehensive data security and protection requirements, including encryption and access controls?
questions.description: DORA Article 29(1)(h) mandates detailed security requirements for data protection throughout the service lifecycle.

questions.question_text: Do you implement logical and/or physical data segregation to protect financial entity data from other customers?
questions.description: DORA Article 29(1) requires: Data belonging to different financial entities must be appropriately segregated to prevent unauthorized access or co-mingling.

questions.question_text: Do you provide regular performance reports to financial entities, including SLA compliance and service metrics?
questions.description: DORA Article 29(2) requires ongoing performance monitoring and reporting to demonstrate compliance with contractual obligations.

questions.question_text: Have you designated qualified individuals as points of contact for managing the relationship with financial entities?
questions.description: DORA Article 29(3) requires designated, qualified personnel to manage financial entity relationships and escalations.

questions.question_text: Do your contracts include provisions to adapt services in response to regulatory changes affecting financial entities?
questions.description: DORA Article 29(4) requires flexibility to accommodate regulatory changes and evolving supervisory expectations.

questions.question_text: Do your contracts explicitly grant competent authorities the right to access, inspect, and audit your ICT systems and premises?
questions.description: DORA Article 29(5) ensures supervisory authorities can directly assess ICT third-party service providers.

questions.question_text: Do you maintain a comprehensive register of all contractual arrangements with financial entities for ICT services?
questions.description: DORA Article 30(1) requires financial entities to maintain a register of information on all ICT third-party service providers.
required_evidence: Register of information on ICT third-party providers

regulatory_source.requirement: Register of information on ICT third-party service providers

questions.question_text: Does your register include detailed information on services provided, including criticality classification and geographic locations?
questions.description: DORA Article 30(2) specifies minimum information requirements for the register, including service descriptions and criticality.
required_evidence: Register of information on ICT third-party providers

regulatory_source.requirement: Register of information on ICT third-party service providers

questions.question_text: Does the register include comprehensive information about ICT third-party service providers, including legal entity details and contact information?
questions.description: DORA Article 30(2) requires: The register must contain provider identification, legal structure, and contact details for relationship management.
required_evidence: Register of information on ICT third-party providers

regulatory_source.requirement: Register of information on ICT third-party service providers

questions.question_text: Does the register document contract start and end dates, notice periods, and renewal terms for all ICT service arrangements?
questions.description: DORA Article 30(2) requires: Register must track contractual timelines, notice periods, and renewal provisions to manage lifecycle and plan exits.
required_evidence: Register of information on ICT third-party providers

regulatory_source.requirement: Register of information on ICT third-party service providers

questions.question_text: Does the register specify the nature and location of data being processed, stored, or accessed by ICT third-party providers?
questions.description: DORA Article 30(2) requires: Register must document data types, processing activities, and geographic locations for compliance and risk management.
required_evidence: Register of information on ICT third-party providers

regulatory_source.requirement: Register of information on ICT third-party service providers

questions.question_text: Does the register identify all subcontractors involved in ICT service delivery, including their locations and roles?
questions.description: DORA Article 30(2) requires: Register must track subcontracting chains to manage concentration risk and ensure oversight of entire service delivery.
required_evidence: Register of information on ICT third-party providers

regulatory_source.requirement: Register of information on ICT third-party service providers

questions.question_text: Do you promptly notify financial entities of material changes to services, subcontractors, or data processing locations?
questions.description: DORA Article 30(3) requires timely notification of changes that could impact risk profiles or compliance.
required_evidence: Register of information on ICT third-party providers

regulatory_source.requirement: Register of information on ICT third-party service providers

questions.question_text: Do you ensure the register is updated at least annually and whenever significant changes occur?
questions.description: DORA Article 30(3) requires: Register must be kept current through regular updates and event-driven updates for significant changes.
required_evidence: Register of information on ICT third-party providers

regulatory_source.requirement: Register of information on ICT third-party service providers

questions.question_text: Has your organization conducted a comprehensive DORA readiness assessment and developed an implementation plan for compliance by January 17, 2025?
questions.description: All ICT third-party service providers to financial entities must be DORA-ready by the January 2025 deadline.

questions.question_text: Have you trained relevant personnel on DORA requirements and their implications for ICT service delivery to financial entities?
questions.description: Staff must understand DORA obligations to ensure proper implementation and ongoing compliance.

questions.question_text: Have you aligned your DORA compliance efforts with NIS2 Directive requirements to ensure comprehensive cybersecurity resilience?
questions.description: DORA and NIS2 have overlapping requirements for ICT risk management, incident reporting, and supply chain security.

questions.question_text: If designated as a critical ICT third-party service provider, have you established processes to comply with the Oversight Framework under DORA Article 31-40?
questions.description: DORA Article 28(1) requires: Critical providers face additional oversight by European Supervisory Authorities (ESAs), including supervisory fees and direct supervision.

questions.question_text: Do you conduct regular ICT business continuity and disaster recovery tests, including scenarios relevant to financial entity operations?
questions.description: DORA requires regular testing of continuity plans, with financial entity participation where appropriate.

questions.question_text: Have you established ICT incident classification criteria aligned with DORA's definition of major ICT-related incidents?
questions.description: DORA defines major incidents based on impact criteria, requiring classification frameworks that trigger appropriate reporting.

questions.question_text: Can you meet DORA's incident reporting timelines: initial notification (significant incident determination), intermediate report (72 hours), and final report (1 month)?
questions.description: DORA Article 19 specifies strict reporting timelines for major ICT-related incidents to competent authorities.

questions.question_text: Do you support Threat-Led Penetration Testing (TLPT) as required by DORA Article 26 for critical ICT services?
questions.description: DORA requires advanced testing including TLPT for critical functions, simulating real-world cyber attack scenarios.

questions.question_text: Do you participate in information sharing arrangements to exchange cyber threat intelligence and best practices with financial entities?
questions.description: DORA Article 45 encourages information sharing to enhance collective cyber resilience across the financial sector.

questions.question_text: Does your governance framework include board-level oversight of ICT risks and third-party dependencies relevant to financial entity services?
questions.description: DORA emphasizes management body responsibility for ICT risk, including third-party risk management.

questions.question_text: Have you documented comprehensive ICT third-party risk management policies aligned with DORA requirements?
questions.description: DORA requires formal documentation of third-party risk management policies, procedures, and controls.

questions.question_text: Have you established key performance indicators (KPIs) and key risk indicators (KRIs) to monitor ICT third-party risk management effectiveness?
questions.description: DORA Article 28 requires: Effective risk management requires measurable indicators to track performance and identify emerging risks.

questions.question_text: Have you assessed whether financial entities face concentration risk due to reliance on your services, particularly if you provide critical or important functions?
questions.description: DORA Article 28(9) requires assessment of concentration risk where multiple financial entities depend on the same ICT third-party service provider.

questions.question_text: Do you identify and disclose interconnected dependencies with other ICT service providers that could amplify systemic risk?
questions.description: DORA Article 28(5) requires: Concentration risk extends beyond single provider dependency to include interconnected chains of ICT service providers.

questions.question_text: Have you established criteria to help financial entities classify your ICT services as critical or important functions under DORA?
questions.description: DORA Article 28(6) requires: Financial entities must classify ICT third-party services as critical/important. Providers should facilitate this with clear service descriptions and impact assessments.

questions.question_text: Do your contracts include provisions for managing planned changes to ICT services, including advance notification and financial entity approval requirements?
questions.description: DORA Article 29(6) requires: Contracts must address how service changes, updates, and upgrades are managed to prevent disruption and maintain security.

questions.subcategory: Liability and Insurance - Limitation of Liability
questions.question_text: Do your contracts include appropriate liability provisions that do not unduly limit financial entity rights in case of service failures or breaches?
questions.description: DORA requires balanced contractual terms that protect financial entity interests while recognizing provider realities.

questions.question_text: Do your contracts clearly establish that financial entities retain full ownership of their data and intellectual property?
questions.description: DORA Article 29(8) requires: Financial entities must maintain ownership and control over their data, with no claims by ICT providers.

questions.question_text: Do your contracts include commitments regarding your own business continuity and disaster recovery capabilities?
questions.description: DORA Article 29(9) requires: Financial entities need assurance of provider resilience through documented BCM/DR capabilities and testing.

questions.question_text: Do your contracts permit financial entities to conduct security assessments, penetration testing, and vulnerability scanning of your services?
questions.description: DORA Article 29(10) requires: Financial entities need the right to independently verify security controls through testing activities.

questions.question_text: Do your contracts include appropriate remedies, such as service credits or performance penalties, for SLA breaches?
questions.description: DORA Article 29(11) requires: Contracts should include financial remedies for service level failures to incentivize performance.

questions.question_text: Do your contracts specify reasonable notice periods for termination that allow adequate time for transition planning?
questions.description: DORA Article 29(9) requires appropriate notice periods to enable orderly exits without disruption.
required_evidence: Business continuity plans for provider exit

questions.question_text: Is the register of ICT third-party arrangements regularly reported to the management body for oversight and decision-making?
questions.description: DORA Article 30 requires board-level awareness and oversight of ICT third-party dependencies.
required_evidence: Register of information on ICT third-party providers

regulatory_source.requirement: Register of information on ICT third-party service providers

questions.question_text: Have you implemented comprehensive ICT security measures aligned with international standards (e.g., ISO 27001, NIST CSF) for services provided to financial entities?
questions.description: DORA requires robust cybersecurity controls proportionate to the criticality of ICT services provided.
required_evidence: ISO 27001 certification or equivalent security standard

questions.question_text: Do you maintain a robust vulnerability management program including timely patching and remediation for services supporting financial entities?
questions.description: DORA Article 28 requires: Critical vulnerabilities must be identified and remediated promptly to protect financial entity data and operations.

questions.question_text: Do you enforce multi-factor authentication (MFA) for all privileged access and remote access to systems processing financial entity data?
questions.description: DORA Article 28 requires: Strong authentication is essential to prevent unauthorized access to sensitive financial data and systems.

questions.question_text: Do you encrypt financial entity data both in transit and at rest using industry-standard encryption algorithms?
questions.description: DORA Article 28 requires: Encryption is mandatory for protecting sensitive financial data from unauthorized access and breaches.

questions.subcategory: Logging and Monitoring - Security Event Logging
questions.question_text: Do you maintain comprehensive security event logs with adequate retention periods to support incident investigation and forensics?
questions.description: DORA requires robust logging and monitoring capabilities to detect, investigate, and respond to security incidents.

questions.question_text: Do you implement regular automated backups of financial entity data with offsite or cloud-based storage for disaster recovery?
questions.description: DORA Article 28 requires: Reliable backups are essential for data recovery and business continuity in case of incidents or disasters.

questions.question_text: Do you regularly test backup restoration procedures to verify data recoverability and meet RTO/RPO requirements?
questions.description: DORA Article 28 requires: Untested backups cannot be relied upon. Regular testing ensures backups can be restored within agreed timeframes.

questions.question_text: Do you maintain visibility into your subcontractors' subcontracting arrangements (fourth parties) that support financial entity services?
questions.description: DORA Article 28 requires: Supply chain risk extends beyond direct subcontractors to include the entire dependency chain.

questions.question_text: Can you accommodate financial entity requirements for data processing and storage to remain within specific geographic jurisdictions (e.g., EU/EEA)?
questions.description: DORA Article 28 requires: Some financial entities may require data to remain within specific jurisdictions for legal or regulatory reasons.

questions.question_text: Do you maintain a process to monitor and implement DORA regulatory updates, EBA/ESMA guidelines, and other relevant EU financial regulations?
questions.description: DORA Article 28 requires: Ongoing compliance requires tracking regulatory developments and updating services accordingly.

questions.question_text: Do you conduct appropriate background checks and screening for personnel with access to financial entity data and systems?
questions.description: DORA Article 28 requires: Personnel security is critical for protecting sensitive financial data and preventing insider threats.

questions.question_text: If you use cloud infrastructure providers, do you support multi-cloud or hybrid cloud deployments to mitigate concentration risk?
questions.description: DORA Article 28 requires: Reliance on a single cloud provider creates concentration risk. Multi-cloud capabilities enhance resilience.

questions.question_text: Do contractual arrangements include provisions for supervisory authority (competent authorities) to have effective access rights to data, premises, and personnel as required by DORA Article 29(1)(h) and Article 28(3)(i)?
questions.description: DORA Article 28(3)(i) requires that contractual arrangements ensure competent authorities can effectively exercise their right of access to data and premises. This is critical for regulatory oversight of ICT third-party providers supporting critical functions.
